user_story: As a subscriber, I want a top-level Fuel page with tabs for Schedule, Calendar, History, and Progress so that I can navigate between different views of my fitness data.
expected_behaviour: On initial render the page shows a hero heading (hidden on mobile, replaced with a compact eyebrow + CTA row) and defaults to the 'Schedule' tab. Four tabs are rendered in a border-b tab strip: Schedule, Calendar (which fetches done-count via GET /api/v1/fuel/workouts/count/?status=done), History (shows done-count badge on sm+), and Progress. Clicking a tab switches content instantly (client-side state only; no navigation). The active tab is underlined with a 1.5px accent bar. The done-count badge only appears on sm+ viewports. No loading state for the tabs themselves.
inventory_notes: Tab state is local React state — refreshing the page always resets to 'schedule'. doneCount used solely for History badge display.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want a prominent 'Log workout' button at the top of the Fuel page so that I can quickly open the new-workout creation flow from anywhere on the page.
expected_behaviour: Two visually identical 'Log workout' buttons render: one in the mobile compact header (sm:hidden), one in the desktop hero (hidden sm:flex). Clicking either opens the NewWorkoutDialog with no preset date (generic quick-add). The button is a rounded-full accent-colored pill with a + icon, minimum 44px touch target. No loading or disabled state on this button itself.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to see my fitness profile summary at the top of Fuel so that I can confirm the assistant knows my level, goals, and equipment.
expected_behaviour: On load a skeleton (SkelBar) renders while useFuelProfileQuery fetches GET /api/v1/fuel/profile/. If profile is null the card is not shown. If onboarding_status is 'pending', the card is suppressed (settings page handles messaging). If 'declined', a brief prose message renders. If 'in_progress', an accent-bordered prompt to continue chatting renders. If 'completed', a compact row shows fitness_level, days_per_week, goals, equipment, and limitations. No edit affordance on this card — edits happen via the assistant.
inventory_notes: Suppressed while 'pending'; settings page is the canonical onboarding surface.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want a 'This Week' summary strip above the tabs so that I can see at a glance how many sessions and minutes I've completed this week.
expected_behaviour: Fetches GET /api/v1/fuel/weekly-summary/ (useWeeklyVolumeQuery). A skeleton renders during load. If no sessions this week (totals.sessions === 0) or data is null, nothing renders. Otherwise shows session count, total minutes, and per-category colored dot + label + count chips. Read-only; no interactions.
inventory_notes: Hidden entirely when zero sessions — not shown as empty state.

feature: Today's Check-In Form (Weight, Sleep, Resting HR)
user_story: As a subscriber, I want a daily check-in card to log today's body weight, sleep hours/quality, and resting heart rate so that my assistant has current biometric data.
expected_behaviour: Loads data from GET /api/v1/fuel/body-weight/, GET /api/v1/fuel/sleep/, and GET /api/v1/fuel/resting-hr/ in parallel. Default date is today; user can pick a past date via a date popover (max=today). When both weight and sleep are logged for the selected date and it is today, the card collapses to a one-liner summary with an 'Edit' button. In expanded form, weight (decimal) and sleep hours (decimal) inputs are side-by-side; a quality dot-picker (1–5 dots) appears when hours are non-empty; resting HR is hidden by default behind a '+ Resting HR' text button. Submit is disabled unless at least one field is dirty (tolerance checks prevent re-saves of unchanged values). On submit, all dirty fields are mutated in parallel via POST or PATCH to their respective endpoints. Saving disables the button and shows 'Saving…'. Fields show 'Logged ✓' emerald badge when an entry exists for the selected date.
entry_points: frontend/components/fuel/today-checkin.tsx:TodayCheckIn ; GET /api/v1/fuel/body-weight/ ; POST /api/v1/fuel/body-weight/ ; PATCH /api/v1/fuel/body-weight/{id}/ ; GET /api/v1/fuel/sleep/ ; POST /api/v1/fuel/sleep/ ; PATCH /api/v1/fuel/sleep/{id}/ ; GET /api/v1/fuel/resting-hr/ ; POST /api/v1/fuel/resting-hr/ ; PATCH /api/v1/fuel/resting-hr/{id}/
inventory_notes: Weight stored as kg, displayed in user's preferred unit (kg/lbs). Quality dot toggle: clicking the active dot clears it. The 'forceEdit' state keeps the card expanded after a save if user clicked 'Edit' on the collapsed summary. Date picker has max=today to prevent future check-ins.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to see a banner when I have unsaved workout drafts from sessions that were deleted mid-edit so that I can recover my data.
expected_behaviour: Reads localStorage key 'nbhd_fuel_orphan_drafts_v1' via listOrphans(tenantId). Listens for both the window 'storage' event (cross-tab) and a custom 'nbhd:orphan-drafts-changed' event (same-tab stash). Renders nothing if no orphans. With orphans, renders an amber-bordered banner showing the first orphan's activity name and date. A 'Recover' button opens the OrphanRecoveryPanel for that draft. If >1 orphan, a <details> element expands to list all drafts with individual OPEN buttons.
inventory_notes: All data is localStorage-only; no backend fetch until the user opens a recovery panel.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want a guided recovery dialog for an orphaned draft so that I can choose to save it as a new workout, merge it with an existing same-day session, or discard it.
expected_behaviour: Opens as a bottom-sheet (mobile) / centered modal (sm+). Immediately fetches GET /api/v1/fuel/workouts/?date_from={date}&date_to={date} to check what's already on that day. Shows a draft summary with activity, metrics, and a read-only detail preview (WorkoutDetailReadOnly). Then routes to one of three cases: (1) 'save_as_new' — no same-day workout; offers 'Save as a new workout' (POST /api/v1/fuel/workouts/) or 'Discard'. (2) 'fill_in' — a planned same-day workout exists; offers 'Apply & mark complete' (PATCH /api/v1/fuel/workouts/{id}/ + marks done), 'Save as a separate workout', or 'Discard'. (3) 'potential_dup' — a completed same-day workout exists; offers 'Same — discard draft', 'Different — save as 2nd workout', or conditionally 'Merge my notes into the existing one' (only if draft has notes and existing doesn't). On success, discardOrphan() removes the stash from localStorage and emits a success toast.
inventory_notes: pickAction logic determines which case applies. The merge-notes path only appears when draft has notes AND existing record has no notes. Recovery uses draft.status to avoid silently downgrading a planned edit to done.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want a 7-day schedule view so that I can see all upcoming and today's planned sessions at a glance.
expected_behaviour: Fetches GET /api/v1/fuel/workouts/?window=7d (useScheduleWindowQuery). Shows a skeleton NextUpSkeleton while isPending and no cache. On desktop (sm+), renders 7 DayCard components in a responsive grid (2-up, 3-up, 4-up). On mobile, renders today as a DayCard + the other 6 days as compact DayRow components. Sessions within each day are sorted by scheduled_at (nulls last) then created_at. An empty day shows 'Rest day · tap to add' dashed button. A background 'syncing…' label appears when isLoading but data is already present.
inventory_notes: DayRow on mobile shows only the first session; multiple sessions are indicated by '+N more'. DayCard shows all sessions as SessionCard rows.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want a 'Next Up' banner highlighting my next planned session so that I can quickly complete or skip it.
expected_behaviour: Derived from the same 7-day schedule data — picks the chronologically first planned session. Renders a card with a left-border accent stripe in the category color. A relative-time label ('in 30 min', 'overdue', 'was Mon 9:00 AM') updates every 30 seconds via setInterval. Three actions: 'Complete' (POST /api/v1/fuel/workouts/{id}/complete/ → on success flashes a '✓ Done' green pill for 1.4s before the card transitions away), 'Skip' (window.prompt for optional reason → POST /api/v1/fuel/workouts/{id}/skip/), and 'View' (opens WorkoutDetail, desktop-only). The entire card is also a tap target to open WorkoutDetail, disabled during the post-complete flash. The skeleton (NextUpSkeleton) renders only while isPending and no cached data.
inventory_notes: The 'overdue' state is shown in amber text. justCompleted state prevents accidental re-open of a just-completed workout. Skip reason is captured via native window.prompt (not a custom modal).

feature: Schedule Tab — Session Card Quick Actions Menu
user_story: As a subscriber, I want a ⋯ menu on each session card so that I can quickly mark a session complete, skip it, or delete it without opening the full detail drawer.
expected_behaviour: Each SessionCard in the 7-day view has a ⋯ button (aria-label='Session actions', aria-expanded). Clicking opens a floating popover menu positioned below the button. A transparent click-away overlay closes it. For 'planned' sessions, 'Mark complete' (POST /api/v1/fuel/workouts/{id}/complete/) and 'Skip…' (window.prompt + POST /api/v1/fuel/workouts/{id}/skip/) items are shown. 'Delete' (with window.confirm) is always available. In-flight actions disable with opacity-50. The menu closes on action completion.
inventory_notes: Mark complete and Skip only appear for status='planned'. Done/skipped sessions only show Delete. Skip reason via native window.prompt; delete confirmation via window.confirm.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want a monthly calendar view showing workout dots on each day so that I can see my training patterns over a month.
expected_behaviour: Fetches GET /api/v1/fuel/calendar/?year={y}&month={m} (useFuelCalendarQuery). Renders a 7-column grid starting Monday. Out-of-month cells are dimmed and clickable. Today's cell has an accent border and 'TODAY' label. Each cell shows a workout count badge and up to 3 colored activity slivers (mobile: dots only; sm+: dots + truncated activity name). A rest day shows a grey dot + 'Rest' label (sm+). Clicking any cell calls onSelectDay which opens the DayDrawer. A 'THIS WEEK' stat (count + hours of done sessions in the past 7 days) is computed client-side. Prev/next month buttons (aria-label) navigate the cursor; a 'TODAY' text button resets to the current month. A color legend renders below the grid.
inventory_notes: Calendar data is also the backing store for the DayDrawer (deduped by React Query). week total counts only 'done' sessions. Overflow items beyond 3 per cell show '+N' label.

feature: Day Drawer — Open Day Detail Panel
user_story: As a subscriber, I want to click a calendar day and see all planned and completed sessions for that day in a side panel so that I can review and manage that day's activity.
expected_behaviour: Renders as a fixed full-screen overlay with a right-aligned panel (full width on mobile, 540px on sm+). Derives session data from the existing calendar cache (no new fetch). Shows a long-format date heading and a subtitle ('No activity', 'Rest day', or 'X done · Y planned'). Planned sessions are listed in a 'PLANNED' section, completed in a 'COMPLETED' section, each as WorkoutRow buttons. Clicking a WorkoutRow opens the WorkoutDetail drawer. An 'Add workout on {date}' dashed button opens NewWorkoutDialog with that date preset. A 'Mark as rest day' button (POST /api/v1/fuel/workouts/ with status='rest') only appears when no done sessions and no existing rest entry. If a rest entry exists, a 'Remove' button (DELETE /api/v1/fuel/workouts/{id}/) removes it. Prev/next day arrows navigate by ±1 day (client-side ISO date arithmetic). Clicking the backdrop closes.
inventory_notes: Day navigation updates the ISO date in the parent page state (setDayIso). The 'Mark as rest day' button is hidden once any 'done' workout exists to prevent marking a workout day as rest.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to pick a workout category when creating a new session so that the detail fields match my activity type.
expected_behaviour: Opens as a right-side panel (full width on mobile, 480px on sm+) with a 'NEW WORKOUT · PICK CATEGORY' header. Step 0 shows a 2-column grid of category buttons (strength, cardio, hiit, calisthenics, mobility, sport, other), each with a colored icon, label, and hint text. Clicking a category sets the category state and advances to step 1. A back arrow appears on step 1 to return to step 0. On a generic 'Log workout' open (no presetDate), if localStorage contains an unfinished draft (loadDraft), a 'Unfinished workout from X ago' restore prompt is shown with Resume / Start fresh buttons. On presetDate opens, restore prompt is suppressed.
inventory_notes: Autosave begins after category is selected. Dialog remounts each time it opens (key={open}) so state fully resets.

feature: New Workout Dialog — Activity Details & Create (Step 1)
user_story: As a subscriber, I want to enter an activity name, date, and status for a new workout so that I can log or plan a session quickly before filling in full details.
expected_behaviour: Step 1 shows: (1) an Activity text input with suggestion chips from CATEGORIES[cat].suggest; (2) if the user has templates for this category (GET /api/v1/fuel/templates/?category={cat}), a 'FROM TEMPLATE' section with buttons that pre-fill activity name and detail_json; (3) a date picker (defaults to presetDate or today); (4) a Done/Planned toggle (default: 'planned' if date is future, 'done' otherwise). 'Create workout' button calls POST /api/v1/fuel/workouts/ with default duration_minutes=45. On success: closes dialog, clears autosave draft, and immediately opens the WorkoutDetail drawer for the new workout via onCreated(result.id). Save button disabled and shows 'Creating…' while pending. On error, React Query's default error handling applies.
inventory_notes: Autosave is debounced 800ms and flushes on pagehide/visibilitychange. Activity field accepts free text; if empty, falls back to CATEGORIES[cat].label on create. default duration_minutes=45 is always submitted — user edits it in the WorkoutDetail drawer.

feature: Workout Detail Drawer — View & Edit Mode
user_story: As a subscriber, I want to open a full-detail panel for any workout so that I can view and edit its metrics, notes, category, date, duration, and RPE.
expected_behaviour: Fetches GET /api/v1/fuel/workouts/{id}/ (useWorkoutQuery, refetchOnMount=always). Shows a skeleton while loading. In view mode, shows activity name, category badge (PLANNED/DONE/SKIPPED), date, duration, RPE, category-specific detail section, and notes. An EDIT button in the header switches to edit mode. In edit mode, activity is an editable input; category shows a horizontal chip switcher; date, duration, RPE are editable fields; category-specific editors render. Save triggers PATCH /api/v1/fuel/workouts/{id}/ and exits edit mode. 'Mark complete' triggers POST /api/v1/fuel/workouts/{id}/complete/ (passes notes, RPE, duration from draft). Planned workouts open directly in edit mode. A stale-edit warning banner appears if the assistant wrote a newer fuel_version while the user was editing; a 'Refresh' button re-fetches. On save error, an alert banner shows the error message and keeps the drawer open with the draft intact.
dependencies: useWorkoutQuery, useUpdateWorkoutMutation, useCompleteWorkoutMutation, useFuelVersionQuery (polls every 30s), useAcquireEditLockMutation, useReleaseEditLockMutation, useMeQuery
inventory_notes: Edit lock: POST /api/v1/fuel/workouts/{id}/edit-lock/ is called on edit-mode entry and heartbeated every 30s; DELETE on exit. Lock is best-effort — missed release self-expires server-side. Autosave debounces dirty edits to localStorage every 800ms; flushes on pagehide/visibilitychange. A recover prompt appears on open if a saved draft differs from server state. A 'last_edited_by_user_at' footnote renders when a done workout was edited.

feature: Workout Detail Drawer — Autosave Draft Recovery
user_story: As a subscriber, I want to be offered my unsaved edits when I reopen a workout I was editing so that I don't lose work from a tab close or navigation away.
expected_behaviour: On WorkoutDetail open, after the server state loads, loadDraft(tenantId, workoutId) checks localStorage for a saved draft. If found and it differs from the server state (sameDraft check), a 'Unsaved changes from X ago' banner renders with 'Restore' and 'Discard' buttons. Restore: sets draft state to saved payload and enters edit mode. Discard: clears the localStorage entry. Autosave writes to localStorage at 800ms debounce while editing and dirty; clears the entry when draft matches server or on successful save.
inventory_notes: Draft recovery is skipped if the saved draft is identical to the server state (avoids prompting unnecessarily). The banner is suppressed while a restore prompt is showing (prevents clobbering).

user_story: As a subscriber, I want a clear message and safe close path when a workout I'm viewing has been deleted by my assistant so that I'm not stuck in a broken state.
expected_behaviour: If GET /api/v1/fuel/workouts/{id}/ returns 404, isNotFoundError triggers: (1) invalidates fuel-workouts, fuel-calendar, fuel-schedule queries; (2) stashes any meaningful in-progress draft to orphan-drafts localStorage (stashOrphan); (3) renders PhantomWorkoutRecovery panel with 'This session was removed' message and a 'Back to Fuel' button that calls onClose(). The OrphanDraftsBanner on the main page then surfaces the stash for recovery.
inventory_notes: Stash is a one-shot side-effect — stashedRef.current prevents double-stash. source field is 'phantom_404' or 'mutation_404' for debugging.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to log exercises with sets and reps (or holds or bodyweight) for a strength workout so that I can track my volume and estimated 1RM per lift.
expected_behaviour: In edit mode, shows an 'EXERCISES' section with '+ EXERCISE' button. Each exercise has a name input and a list of sets. Each set has a MetricToggle (WEIGHT / BODY / HOLD) to switch between weighted_reps, bodyweight_reps, and hold_time. Weighted: reps + weight (in user's unit, stored as kg). Bodyweight: reps only. Hold: hold_s (seconds). A trash button deletes individual sets or the whole exercise. '+ SET' adds a set defaulting to the last set's weight. Estimated 1RM is computed client-side (Epley formula) for weighted sets and shown as 'est 1RM X unit' if >0. Stored in detail_json.exercises. No server call per keystroke — all edits are staged in local draft state until 'Save changes' is clicked.
inventory_notes: NumericInput holds string during editing, commits on blur/Enter. PR flag is display-only (set by backend/assistant); not user-editable in this editor.
